title: Tanker Shipping - April 21, 2026
summary: The tanker market is facing significant disruption as the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz forces a record migration of VLCCs into the Atlantic, creating an oversupply that is weighing heavily on Western freight rates. Meanwhile, oil prices are projected to remain near $100 per barrel for the rest of 2026 due to tightening fundamentals and anticipated inventory drawdowns. Despite current positive sentiment, a rising vessel orderbook suggests a potential long-term market downcycle as new supply eventually begins to outpace global demand.

children.title: Overview
children.summary:
- Straits Still Shut as Negotiations Continue - Headlines remain mixed and
- We now Expect Oil Prices to Remain ~$100/bbl for the Rest of the Year - Reuters: BWET.K
- Our Long-term View - The tanker market has been recovering from a long period
